name: Laniakea Beach
tagline: Known as Turtle Beach — where Hawaiian green sea turtles haul out on the sand right in front of you.
parking: Small roadside lot on Kamehameha Hwy; very limited, watch for traffic pulling in
bestTime: Late morning to early afternoon for best turtle sightings; calmer summer months for swimming
talkStory: Laniakea means 'wide sky' in Hawaiian, but everyone calls it Turtle Beach. Hawaiian green sea turtles (honu) haul out on this sand to bask almost every afternoon, drawn by the algae-rich reef just offshore. Volunteers from Malama na Honu stand guard daily to keep people at the legally required distance. The turtles here are protected under both federal law and Hawaiian cultural tradition — honu are considered aumakua (family guardians) by many Hawaiian families. Quick stop on the drive to or from Haleiwa, but never touch the turtles.
